args.command: mkdir -p /workspace/research /workspace/decisions && echo "Workspace ready"

cat > /workspace/research/notes.md << 'NOTES'
# Research Notes — Password Manager Evaluation

## Date: 2025-06-18

## Products to Evaluate
1. 1Password (Teams/Business)
2. Bitwarden (Teams/Business)
3. LastPass (Business)
4. Dashlane (Business)
5. Keeper (Business)

## Criteria to Research
- Per-seat pricing (annual)
- SSO integration (Google Workspace, Okta, etc.)
- Audit logging
- SOC 2 / FedRAMP compliance
- Mobile + desktop coverage
- Developer tool integrations (CLI, CI/CD, browser extensions)
- Admin console UX
- Security incident history
- Onboarding/migration tools

## Methodology
- Visit each product's pricing page
- Visit each product's trust/compliance page
- Check GitHub for CLI tools
- Search for security incidents
- Cross-reference with independent reviews
NOTES
echo "Notes initialized"

# 1Password - Pricing page
curl -sL "https://1password.com/pricing/" -o /tmp/1password-pricing.html 2>&1
echo "1Password pricing: $(wc -c < /tmp/1password-pricing.html) bytes"

# 1Password - Trust/Security page
curl -sL "https://1password.com/trust/" -o /tmp/1password-trust.html 2>&1
echo "1Password trust: $(wc -c < /tmp/1password-trust.html) bytes"

# 1Password - Security whitepaper
curl -sL "https://1password.com/security/" -o /tmp/1password-security.html 2>&1
echo "1Password security: $(wc -c < /tmp/1password-security.html) bytes"

# Extract pricing info from 1Password page
grep -i -E 'price|per user|per seat|monthly|annual|\$[0-9]' /tmp/1password-pricing.html | head -40

# Try to find pricing data in the HTML
grep -oP '\$[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?' /tmp/1password-pricing.html | sort -u | head -20

# Look for pricing context around those numbers
grep -oP '.{0,100}\$11\.99.{0,100}' /tmp/1password-pricing.html | head -5
echo "---"
grep -oP '.{0,100}\$4\.99.{0,100}' /tmp/1password-pricing.html | head -5
echo "---"
grep -oP '.{0,100}\$29\.95.{0,100}' /tmp/1password-pricing.html | head -5

# Get more context on 1Password Business pricing
grep -oP '.{0,200}Business.{0,200}priceAnnual.{0,100}' /tmp/1password-pricing.html | head -3
echo "==="
# Also look for "Teams" plan
grep -oP '.{0,200}Teams.{0,200}priceAnnual.{0,100}' /tmp/1password-pricing.html | head -3

# Bitwarden pricing
curl -sL "https://bitwarden.com/pricing/" -o /tmp/bitwarden-pricing.html 2>&1
echo "Bitwarden pricing: $(wc -c < /tmp/bitwarden-pricing.html) bytes"

# Bitwarden trust/security
curl -sL "https://bitwarden.com/trust/" -o /tmp/bitwarden-trust.html 2>&1
echo "Bitwarden trust: $(wc -c < /tmp/bitwarden-trust.html) bytes"

# Extract Bitwarden pricing
grep -oP '\$[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?' /tmp/bitwarden-pricing.html | sort -u | head -20
echo "==="
grep -oP '.{0,150}price.{0,150}' /tmp/bitwarden-pricing.html | grep -i 'annual\|monthly\|per user\|per seat\|business\|teams' | head -10

# Get Bitwarden Business Teams and Enterprise pricing details
grep -oP '.{0,300}Business Teams.{0,300}' /tmp/bitwarden-pricing.html | head -3
echo "==="
grep -oP '.{0,300}Business Enterprise.{0,300}' /tmp/bitwarden-pricing.html | head -3

# LastPass pricing
curl -sL "https://www.lastpass.com/pricing" -o /tmp/lastpass-pricing.html 2>&1
echo "LastPass pricing: $(wc -c < /tmp/lastpass-pricing.html) bytes"

# LastPass trust
curl -sL "https://www.lastpass.com/trust-center" -o /tmp/lastpass-trust.html 2>&1
echo "LastPass trust: $(wc -c < /tmp/lastpass-trust.html) bytes"

# Extract LastPass pricing
grep -oP '\$[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?' /tmp/lastpass-pricing.html | sort -u | head -20
echo "==="
grep -oP '.{0,200}price.{0,200}' /tmp/lastpass-pricing.html | grep -i 'annual\|monthly\|business\|enterprise\|per user\|per seat' | head -10

# LastPass pricing is dynamically loaded. Let's try their API or look for JSON data
grep -oP '"price"[^}]*' /tmp/lastpass-pricing.html | head -10
echo "==="
# Try the LastPass features page for more info
curl -sL "https://www.lastpass.com/features" -o /tmp/lastpass-features.html 2>&1
echo "LastPass features: $(wc -c < /tmp/lastpass-features.html) bytes"

# Try LastPass pricing via their API or look for script data
grep -oP 'LPBusiness[^"]*"[^"]*"[^"]*"[^"]*"' /tmp/lastpass-pricing.html | head -5
echo "==="
# Search for any numeric pricing in the page
grep -oP '[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' /tmp/lastpass-pricing.html | sort -u | head -20

# LastPass pricing is JS-rendered. Let's try a different approach - check their blog or third-party sources
# Also try the LastPass Business page directly
curl -sL "https://www.lastpass.com/business-password-manager" -o /tmp/lastpass-business.html 2>&1
echo "LastPass business: $(wc -c < /tmp/lastpass-business.html) bytes"

# Try to find pricing in any script tags
grep -oP 'data-price[^>]*' /tmp/lastpass-pricing.html | head -10
echo "==="
grep -oP 'amount[^>]*' /tmp/lastpass-pricing.html | head -10

# Let's use a third-party comparison site for LastPass pricing
# Also try G2 or similar
curl -sL "https://www.lastpass.com/pricing" -o /tmp/lastpass-pricing2.html 2>&1
# Look for any JSON-LD or structured data
grep -oP '<script[^>]*type="application/ld\+json"[^>]*>.*?</script>' /tmp/lastpass-pricing.html | head -5
echo "==="
# Try to find pricing in meta tags
grep -i 'price\|cost\|per user' /tmp/lastpass-pricing.html | grep -v 'class\|style\|data-' | head -10

# Let's try a different approach - use a known third-party source for LastPass pricing
# Also try the LastPass comparison page
curl -sL "https://www.lastpass.com/compare-plans" -o /tmp/lastpass-compare.html 2>&1
echo "LastPass compare: $(wc -c < /tmp/lastpass-compare.html) bytes"

# Try to find pricing data in the compare page
grep -oP '\$[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?' /tmp/lastpass-compare.html | sort -u | head -20

# Let's try a well-known comparison site for LastPass pricing
curl -sL "https://www.pcmag.com/picks/the-best-password-managers" -o /tmp/pcmag-password.html 2>&1
echo "PCMag: $(wc -c < /tmp/pcmag-password.html) bytes"

# Also try to get LastPass pricing from a review site
curl -sL "https://www.trustpilot.com/review/www.lastpass.com" -o /tmp/lastpass-trustpilot.html 2>&1
echo "Trustpilot: $(wc -c < /tmp/lastpass-trustpilot.html) bytes"

# Let me try a more direct approach - use curl with a different user agent or try the LastPass API
# Also try the LastPass pricing page with a mobile user agent
curl -sL -A "Mozilla/5.0 (iPhone; CPU iPhone OS 16_0 like Mac OS X)" "https://www.lastpass.com/pricing" -o /tmp/lastpass-pricing-mobile.html 2>&1
echo "LastPass mobile: $(wc -c < /tmp/lastpass-pricing-mobile.html) bytes"

# Try to find pricing in the mobile version
grep -oP '\$[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?' /tmp/lastpass-pricing-mobile.html | sort -u | head -20

# Let me try a different approach - look at the LastPass pricing page source more carefully
# The pricing might be loaded via an API call
grep -oP 'https?://[^"]*pricing[^"]*' /tmp/lastpass-pricing.html | head -10
echo "==="
# Look for any API endpoints
grep -oP 'https?://[^"]*api[^"]*' /tmp/lastpass-pricing.html | head -10
echo "==="
# Look for fetch or axios calls
grep -oP 'fetch\([^)]*\)' /tmp/lastpass-pricing.html | head -5

# Let me try a well-known third-party source for LastPass pricing
# Try the LastPass Wikipedia page
curl -sL "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/LastPass" -o /tmp/lastpass-wiki.html 2>&1
echo "Wiki: $(wc -c < /tmp/lastpass-wiki.html) bytes"

# Try to find pricing info
grep -i 'price\|cost\|per user\|per seat\|monthly\|annual' /tmp/lastpass-wiki.html | head -10

# Let me try a third-party comparison site that has LastPass pricing
curl -sL "https://www.capterra.com/password-management-software/" -o /tmp/capterra.html 2>&1
echo "Capterra: $(wc -c < /tmp/capterra.html) bytes"

# Try a more specific comparison
curl -sL "https://www.softwareadvice.com/password-management/" -o /tmp/softwareadvice.html 2>&1
echo "SoftwareAdvice: $(wc -c < /tmp/softwareadvice.html) bytes"

# Those are too small - likely bot protection. Let me try a different approach.
# Let me use the LastPass pricing page but look for the pricing data in a different way
# Check if there's a pricing API

# Let's try to find the pricing data in the page's JavaScript bundles
grep -oP 'src="[^"]*\.js[^"]*"' /tmp/lastpass-pricing.html | head -10
echo "==="
# Also look for any inline JSON with pricing
grep -oP '"price"[^,]*,[^,]*,[^,]*' /tmp/lastpass-pricing.html | head -10

# Let me try a well-known tech review site for LastPass pricing
curl -sL "https://www.techradar.com/best/best-password-manager" -o /tmp/techradar.html 2>&1
echo "TechRadar: $(wc -c < /tmp/techradar.html) bytes"

# Try to find LastPass pricing from a review
grep -i 'lastpass.*price\|lastpass.*cost\|lastpass.*\$' /tmp/techradar.html | head -5

# Let me try a more targeted approach - search for LastPass pricing on a known source
curl -sL "https://www.pcmag.com/picks/the-best-password-managers" -o /tmp/pcmag2.html 2>&1
echo "PCMag2: $(wc -c < /tmp/pcmag2.html) bytes"

# Search for LastPass pricing in PCMag
grep -i 'lastpass' /tmp/pcmag2.html | grep -i 'price\|cost\|per\|month\|annual' | head -5

# Dashlane pricing
curl -sL "https://www.dashlane.com/pricing" -o /tmp/dashlane-pricing.html 2>&1
echo "Dashlane pricing: $(wc -c < /tmp/dashlane-pricing.html) bytes"

# Dashlane trust
curl -sL "https://www.dashlane.com/security" -o /tmp/dashlane-security.html 2>&1
echo "Dashlane security: $(wc -c < /tmp/dashlane-security.html) bytes"

# Extract Dashlane pricing
grep -oP '\$[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?' /tmp/dashlane-pricing.html | sort -u | head -20
echo "==="
grep -oP '.{0,200}price.{0,200}' /tmp/dashlane-pricing.html | grep -i 'annual\|monthly\|business\|team\|enterprise\|per user\|per seat' | head -10

# Get more Dashlane pricing context
grep -oP '.{0,300}priceSpecification.{0,300}' /tmp/dashlane-pricing.html | head -5
echo "==="
# Look for the plan names and prices
grep -oP '"planSegment":"[^"]*"' /tmp/dashlane-pricing.html | sort -u
echo "==="
grep -oP '"price":"[^"]*"' /tmp/dashlane-pricing.html | sort -u

# Get Dashlane pricing details more carefully
# From the JSON-LD: Password Management = $8/user/month annual, Enterprise = $4/user/month annual
# Let me verify by looking at the full pricing section
grep -oP '"name":"[^"]*Password[^"]*"' /tmp/dashlane-pricing.html | head -10
echo "==="
grep -oP '"name":"[^"]*Business[^"]*"' /tmp/dashlane-pricing.html | head -10
echo "==="
# Look for the full pricing table
grep -oP '"planSegment":"[^"]*".{0,500}' /tmp/dashlane-pricing.html | head -5

# Let me look at the Dashlane pricing more carefully
# From JSON-LD: Password Management = $8/user/month annual, Enterprise = $4/user/month annual
# But the page also shows $11 for business
# Let me look at the full pricing structure
grep -oP 'customPrice":"[^"]*"' /tmp/dashlane-pricing.html | sort -u
echo "==="
# Look for the actual price display
grep -oP '"price":[^,]*' /tmp/dashlane-pricing.html | sort -u

# Let me look at the Dashlane pricing page more carefully
# It seems like: Password Management = $8/user/month, Business = $11/user/month, Enterprise = custom
# Let me verify with the JSON-LD data
python3 -c "
import re
with open('/tmp/dashlane-pricing.html') as f:
    content = f.read()
# Find all JSON-LD offers
offers = re.findall(r'\"offers\"[^}]*?\"price\":\s*\"([^\"]+)\"[^}]*?\"priceCurrency\":\s*\"([^\"]+)\"[^}]*?\"unitText\":\s*\"([^\"]+)\"', content)
for offer in offers:
    print(f'Price: {offer[0]}, Currency: {offer[1]}, Unit: {offer[2]}')
"

# Let me try a different approach for Dashlane pricing
python3 -c "
import re
with open('/tmp/dashlane-pricing.html') as f:
    content = f.read()
# Find all price mentions in JSON-LD
matches = re.findall(r'\"price\":\s*\"([^\"]+)\"', content)
print('All price values:', set(matches))
# Find the structured data
matches2 = re.findall(r'\"name\":\s*\"([^\"]+)\".*?\"price\":\s*\"([^\"]+)\"', content, re.DOTALL)
for name, price in matches2[:10]:
    print(f'Name: {name}, Price: {price}')
"

# The HTML is escaped. Let me handle that
python3 -c "
import re
with open('/tmp/dashlane-pricing.html') as f:
    content = f.read()
# Unescape HTML entities
content = content.replace('"', '\"').replace('&', '&').replace('<', '<').replace('>', '>')
# Find all price mentions
matches = re.findall(r'\"price\":\s*\"([^\"]+)\"', content)
print('All price values:', set(matches))
# Find structured data with context
matches2 = re.findall(r'\"name\":\s*\"([^\"]{0,100})\".*?\"price\":\s*\"([^\"]+)\"', content, re.DOTALL)
for name, price in matches2[:10]:
    print(f'Name: {name}, Price: {price}')
"

# Good! Dashlane pricing from JSON-LD:
# - Password Management: $8/user/month annual
# - Enterprise: $4/user/month annual (this seems like a starting price)
# - Free trial: $0
# The $11 customPrice is for the Business tier

# Now let's get Keeper pricing
curl -sL "https://www.keepersecurity.com/pricing" -o /tmp/keeper-pricing.html 2>&1
echo "Keeper pricing: $(wc -c < /tmp/keeper-pricing.html) bytes"

# Keeper trust
curl -sL "https://www.keepersecurity.com/trust-center" -o /tmp/keeper-trust.html 2>&1
echo "Keeper trust: $(wc -c < /tmp/keeper-trust.html) bytes"

# Extract Keeper pricing
grep -oP '\$[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?' /tmp/keeper-pricing.html | sort -u | head -20
echo "==="
grep -oP '.{0,200}price.{0,200}' /tmp/keeper-pricing.html | grep -i 'annual\|monthly\|business\|team\|enterprise\|per user\|per seat' | head -10

# Keeper pricing is also JS-rendered. Let me try to find it in the page's data
python3 -c "
import re
with open('/tmp/keeper-pricing.html') as f:
    content = f.read()
# Unescape
content = content.replace('"', '\"').replace('&', '&').replace('<', '<').replace('>', '>')
# Find pricing data

print('All price values:', set(matches))
# Find any numeric pricing
matches2 = re.findall(r'data-product-path=\"([^\"]+)\"', content)
print('Product paths:', matches2)
"

# Let me try a third-party source for Keeper pricing
curl -sL "https://www.keepersecurity.com/business-password-manager" -o /tmp/keeper-business.html 2>&1
echo "Keeper business: $(wc -c < /tmp/keeper-business.html) bytes"

# Try to find pricing in the business page
grep -oP '\$[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?' /tmp/keeper-business.html | sort -u | head -20

# Let me try a well-known comparison site for Keeper pricing

curl -sL "https://www.keepersecurity.com/pricing" -o /tmp/keeper-pricing2.html 2>&1

# Let me also check the Keeper comparison chart
curl -sL "https://www.keepersecurity.com/compare-plans" -o /tmp/keeper-compare.html 2>&1
echo "Keeper compare: $(wc -c < /tmp/keeper-compare.html) bytes"

# Try to find pricing in the compare page
grep -oP '\$[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?' /tmp/keeper-compare.html | sort -u | head -20

# Let me try a different approach - use a known third-party source

# Try to find pricing from a tech review

echo "Keeper SA: $(wc -c < /tmp/keeper-sa.html) bytes"

# Those are too small - bot protection. Let me try a different approach.
# Let me use the Wayback Machine or try a different source

# Try to find Keeper pricing from a blog post or news article
curl -sL "https://www.keepersecurity.com/blog/" -o /tmp/keeper-blog.html 2>&1
echo "Keeper blog: $(wc -c < /tmp/keeper-blog.html) bytes"

# Try to find pricing from a comparison article
curl -sL "https://www.capterra.com/p/156363/Keeper/" -o /tmp/keeper-capterra.html 2>&1
echo "Keeper Capterra: $(wc -c < /tmp/keeper-capterra.html) bytes"

# Let me try a different approach - use the Keeper API or check their pricing page source more carefully
# Also try to find pricing from a known review site

# Try to find Keeper pricing from a tech review
curl -sL "https://www.techradar.com/best/best-password-manager" -o /tmp/techradar2.html 2>&1
echo "TechRadar2: $(wc -c < /tmp/techradar2.html) bytes"

# Search for Keeper pricing in TechRadar
grep -i 'keeper' /tmp/techradar2.html | grep -i 'price\|cost\|per\|month\|annual\|\$' | head -5

# Let me try a more targeted approach - search for Keeper pricing on Google
# Also try to find pricing from a known source

# Try to find Keeper pricing from a comparison site
curl -sL "https://www.pcmag.com/picks/the-best-password-managers" -o /tmp/pcmag3.html 2>&1
echo "PCMag3: $(wc -c < /tmp/pcmag3.html) bytes"

# Search for Keeper pricing in PCMag
grep -i 'keeper' /tmp/pcmag3.html | grep -i 'price\|cost\|per\|month\|annual\|\$' | head -5

# Let me try a more targeted approach for Keeper pricing
# Try to find Keeper pricing from a specific review
curl -sL "https://www.pcmag.com/reviews/keeper-password-manager-and-digital-vault" -o /tmp/keeper-pcmag.html 2>&1
echo "Keeper PCMag: $(wc -c < /tmp/keeper-pcmag.html) bytes"

# Search for Keeper pricing
grep -i 'price\|cost\|per user\|per seat\|monthly\|annual' /tmp/keeper-pcmag.html | grep -i 'keeper\|price\|cost\|\$' | head -10

# Let me search for Keeper pricing in the PCMag review
python3 -c "
import re
with open('/tmp/keeper-pcmag.html') as f:
    content = f.read()
# Find pricing info
matches = re.findall(r'Pricing[^<]*', content)
for m in matches[:10]:
    print(m)
print('---')
# Find dollar amounts near Keeper
matches2 = re.findall(r'.{0,200}\$[0-9]+.{0,200}', content)
for m in matches2[:5]:
    print(m[:200])
"

# Let me try a different approach - search for Keeper pricing on a known source

curl -sL "https://www.zdnet.com/article/best-password-managers/" -o /tmp/zdnet-password.html 2>&1

# Search for Keeper pricing
grep -i 'keeper' /tmp/zdnet-password.html | grep -i 'price\|cost\|per\|month\|annual\|\$' | head -5

# Now let me gather security/compliance data for all products
# 1Password - SOC2, FedRAMP
curl -sL "https://1password.com/trust/" -o /tmp/1password-trust2.html 2>&1
echo "1Password trust: $(wc -c < /tmp/1password-trust2.html) bytes"

# Bitwarden - SOC2, FedRAMP
curl -sL "https://bitwarden.com/compliance/" -o /tmp/bitwarden-compliance.html 2>&1
echo "Bitwarden compliance: $(wc -c < /tmp/bitwarden-compliance.html) bytes"

# LastPass - SOC2, FedRAMP
curl -sL "https://www.lastpass.com/trust-center" -o /tmp/lastpass-trust2.html 2>&1
echo "LastPass trust: $(wc -c < /tmp/lastpass-trust2.html) bytes"

# Extract compliance info

grep -i 'soc 2\|soc2\|fedramp\|iso 27001\|gdpr\|hipaa' /tmp/1password-trust2.html | head -10
echo "=== Bitwarden Compliance ==="
grep -i 'soc 2\|soc2\|fedramp\|iso 27001\|gdpr\|hipaa' /tmp/bitwarden-compliance.html | head -10
echo "=== LastPass Trust ==="
grep -i 'soc 2\|soc2\|fedramp\|iso 27001\|gdpr\|hipaa' /tmp/lastpass-trust2.html | head -10

# Let me get the Bitwarden compliance page content more carefully
python3 -c "
import re
with open('/tmp/bitwarden-compliance.html') as f:
    content = f.read()
# Unescape
content = content.replace('"', '\"').replace('&', '&').replace('<', '<').replace('>', '>')
# Find SOC2, FedRAMP, ISO mentions
matches = re.findall(r'.{0,100}(SOC 2|SOC2|FedRAMP|ISO 27001|GDPR|HIPAA|FIPS).{0,100}', content, re.IGNORECASE)
for m in matches[:10]:
    print(m[:200])
    print('---')
"

# Let me get more context around those compliance mentions
python3 -c "
import re
with open('/tmp/bitwarden-compliance.html') as f:
    content = f.read()
content = content.replace('"', '\"').replace('&', '&').replace('<', '<').replace('>', '>')
# Find compliance badges/certifications
matches = re.findall(r'<[^>]*>{0,500}', content)
# Look for specific compliance text
for term in ['SOC 2', 'FedRAMP', 'ISO 27001', 'GDPR', 'HIPAA', 'FIPS']:
    idx = content.lower().find(term.lower())
    if idx >= 0:
        print(f'--- {term} ---')
        print(content[max(0,idx-100):idx+200])
        print()
" 2>&1 | head -60

# Now let me check for security incidents for each product
# LastPass had major breaches
curl -sL "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/LastPass" -o /tmp/lastpass-wiki2.html 2>&1
echo "LastPass Wiki: $(wc -c < /tmp/lastpass-wiki2.html) bytes"

# Search for breach info
python3 -c "
import re
with open('/tmp/lastpass-wiki2.html') as f:
    content = f.read()
content = content.replace('"', '\"').replace('&', '&').replace('<', '<').replace('>', '>')
# Find breach mentions
for term in ['breach', 'hack', 'compromise', 'incident', '2022', '2024']:
    idx = content.lower().find(term.lower())
    while idx >= 0:
        print(f'--- {term} at {idx} ---')
        print(content[max(0,idx-50):idx+200])
        print()
        idx = content.lower().find(term.lower(), idx+1)
        if idx > 0:
            break  # Just show first occurrence
" 2>&1 | head -60

# Let me get the security incidents section from LastPass Wikipedia
python3 -c "
import re
with open('/tmp/lastpass-wiki2.html') as f:
    content = f.read()
# Find the security incidents section
idx = content.find('Security incidents')
if idx >= 0:
    section = content[idx:idx+3000]
    # Clean up HTML tags
    section = re.sub(r'<[^>]+>', ' ', section)
    section = section.replace('&', '&').replace('<', '<').replace('>', '>').replace('"', '\"')
    print(section[:2000])
"

# Get the actual text of the security incidents
python3 -c "
import re
with open('/tmp/lastpass-wiki2.html') as f:
    content = f.read()
# Find the security incidents section
idx = content.find('Security incidents')
if idx >= 0:
    section = content[idx:idx+5000]
    # Clean up HTML tags
    section = re.sub(r'<[^>]+>', ' ', section)
    section = section.replace('&', '&').replace('<', '<').replace('>', '>').replace('"', '\"')
    section = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', section)
    print(section[:3000])
"

tags after 'Security incidents' idx = content.find('Security incidents') if idx >= 0: section = content[idx:idx+8000] # Extract

content paragraphs = re.findall(r'

([^<]+)', section) for p in paragraphs[:15]: p = p.replace('&', '&').replace('<', '<').replace('>', '>').replace('"', '\"') print(p.strip()) print('---') "

# The wiki uses different paragraph tags. Let me try a different approach

# The wiki structure is different. Let me search for the breach text more broadly
python3 -c "
import re
with open('/tmp/lastpass-wiki2.html') as f:
    content = f.read()
# Search for '2022' in the content
for match in re.finditer(r'2022', content):
    start = max(0, match.start()-100)
    end = min(len(content), match.end()+500)
    text = content[start:end]
    text = re.sub(r'<[^>]+>', ' ', text)
    text = text.replace('&', '&').replace('<', '<').replace('>', '>').replace('"', '\"')
    text = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', text).strip()
    if 'breach' in text.lower() or 'vault' in text.lower() or 'data' in text.lower():
        print(text[:400])
        print('===')
" 2>&1 | head -40

# Check for Bitwarden security incidents

# Check for security incidents in 1Password and Bitwarden wikis
python3 -c "
import re
for product, file in [('1Password', '/tmp/1password-wiki.html'), ('Bitwarden', '/tmp/bitwarden-wiki.html')]:
    with open(file) as f:
        content = f.read()
    # Find security/breach mentions
    for term in ['breach', 'hack', 'compromise', 'incident', 'security']:
        idx = content.lower().find(term.lower())
        if idx >= 0:
            section = content[max(0,idx-50):idx+300]
            section = re.sub(r'<[^>]+>', ' ', section)
            section = section.replace('&', '&').replace('<', '<').replace('>', '>').replace('"', '\"')
            section = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', section).strip()
            print(f'{product} - {term}: {section[:200]}')
            print('---')
" 2>&1 | head -40

# The wiki structure is tricky. Let me search for the actual text content
python3 -c "
import re
with open('/tmp/1password-wiki.html') as f:
    content = f.read()
# Search for 'Okta' in the main content
idx = content.find('mw-parser-output')
if idx >= 0:
    main = content[idx:]
    # Find Okta mentions
    for match in re.finditer(r'Okta', main):
        start = max(0, match.start()-200)
        end = min(len(main), match.end()+500)
        text = main[start:end]
        text = re.sub(r'<[^>]+>', ' ', text)
        text = text.replace('&', '&').replace('<', '<').replace('>', '>').replace('"', '\"')
        text = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', text).strip()
        print(text[:400])
        print('===')
" 2>&1 | head -30

# Check for security incidents in Dashlane and Keeper wikis
python3 -c "
import re
for product, file in [('Dashlane', '/tmp/dashlane-wiki.html'), ('Keeper', '/tmp/keeper-wiki.html')]:
    with open(file) as f:
        content = f.read()
    # Find security/breach mentions
    for term in ['breach', 'hack', 'compromise', 'incident', 'security']:
        idx = content.lower().find(term.lower())
        while idx >= 0:
            section = content[max(0,idx-50):idx+200]
            section = re.sub(r'<[^>]+>', ' ', section)
            section = section.replace('&', '&').replace('<', '<').replace('>', '>').replace('"', '\"')
            section = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', section).strip()
            if len(section) > 30:
                print(f'{product} - {term}: {section[:200]}')
                print('---')
            idx = content.lower().find(term.lower(), idx+1)
            if idx > 0:
                break
" 2>&1 | head -40

# Let me search for the actual breach text in Dashlane
python3 -c "
import re
with open('/tmp/dashlane-wiki.html') as f:
    content = f.read()
idx = content.find('mw-parser-output')
if idx >= 0:
    main = content[idx:]
    # Find breach mentions
    for match in re.finditer(r'breach', main, re.IGNORECASE):
        start = max(0, match.start()-200)
        end = min(len(main), match.end()+500)
        text = main[start:end]
        text = re.sub(r'<[^>]+>', ' ', text)
        text = text.replace('&', '&').replace('<', '<').replace('>', '>').replace('"', '\"')
        text = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', text).strip()
        print(text[:400])
        print('===')
" 2>&1 | head -30

# Try to find pricing in the page's JavaScript
python3 -c "
import re
with open('/tmp/lastpass-pricing3.html') as f:
    content = f.read()
# Look for pricing data in script tags
scripts = re.findall(r'<script[^>]*>(.*?)</script>', content, re.DOTALL)
for script in scripts:
    if 'price' in script.lower() or 'business' in script.lower():
        # Extract any dollar amounts
        prices = re.findall(r'\$[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?', script)
        if prices:
            print('Found prices:', set(prices))
            # Show context
            idx = script.lower().find('business')
            if idx >= 0:
                print(script[max(0,idx-100):idx+200][:300])
" 2>&1 | head -20

# Let me try a different approach - search for LastPass business pricing on a known source

# Search for pricing in the review - look for the actual text content
python3 -c "
import re
with open('/tmp/lastpass-business-pcmag3.html') as f:
    content = f.read()
# Find the main content
idx = content.find('mw-parser-output')
if idx < 0:
    idx = content.find('article')
if idx >= 0:
    section = content[idx:idx+10000]
    # Extract text
    text = re.sub(r'<[^>]+>', ' ', section)
    text = text.replace('&', '&').replace('<', '<').replace('>', '>').replace('"', '\"')
    text = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', text).strip()
    # Find pricing mentions
    for match in re.finditer(r'price|cost|per user|per seat|monthly|annual|\$', text, re.IGNORECASE):
        start = max(0, match.start()-100)
        end = min(len(text), match.end()+200)
        print(text[start:end])
        print('---')
" 2>&1 | head -30
